questions.question: Who is required to connect Asana to Notion AI?

options.text: Any workspace member with a Business plan
options.explanation: You must be a Notion workspace owner to initiate the connection.

options.text: You must be a Notion workspace owner
options.explanation: Only workspace owners are permitted to connect third-party apps like Asana to Notion AI.

options.text: Any user with an Asana Advanced plan
options.explanation: The connection permissions depend on the Notion plan (Business or Enterprise) and the Asana role.

options.text: An Asana Admin or IT Manager
options.explanation: While Asana Enterprise admins manage the connection on their end, the action is initiated by the Notion workspace owner.

questions.question: How do you initiate the connection process in Notion?

options.text: Navigate to Settings > Integrations > Add Asana
options.explanation: The connection must be initiated through the Notion AI interface, not the general Settings menu.

options.text: Open Notion AI, click 'All sources' → 'Connect apps'
options.explanation: This directs you to the correct modal where you select 'Notion AI' and then click the '+' next to Asana.

options.text: Go to the Asana website and click 'Connect with Notion'
options.explanation: The connection must be managed entirely within the Notion application to ensure proper permission mapping.

options.text: Contact Support to request an API connection
options.explanation: The connector is built directly into Notion AI and does not require manual API requests from the user.

questions.question: Which method is required for Asana Enterprise customers to establish the connection?

options.text: Redirect to Asana to click 'Allow'
options.explanation: This method is only used for 'All other plans' (Personal, Starter, Advanced).

options.text: Enter an API token provided by the admin
options.explanation: For Enterprise accounts, admins must generate and input a service account token with full permissions.

options.text: Manually upload a CSV file of tasks
options.explanation: The connector reads data directly from the Asana API; CSV imports are not supported.

options.text: Connect via OAuth 2.0 login
options.explanation: Enterprise connections use API tokens, not the standard OAuth login flow.

questions.question: When connecting using 'All other plans', what is the maximum scope of data accessible?

options.text: All private projects and tasks in the workspace
options.explanation: Notion AI cannot access private projects where the connecting user is not a member.

options.text: Only tasks assigned to the user who created the connection
options.explanation: Users can access tasks they have general permissions for, not just assigned tasks.

options.text: Public projects and private projects the user is a member of
options.explanation: This is the correct scope: public projects plus private projects where the user holds membership.

options.text: No data, as 'All other plans' are currently unsupported
options.explanation: All other plan types (Personal, Starter, Advanced) are fully supported.

questions.question: Can you limit your search scope to only Notion content?

options.text: No, Notion AI searches everything simultaneously
options.explanation: You can toggle the search scope to focus on specific sources.

options.text: Yes, use the 'Find in' toggle to select 'Notion'
options.explanation: The 'Find in' dropdown allows you to switch between 'Everything', 'Notion', and 'Asana'.

options.text: Yes, by unchecking the Asana box in settings
options.explanation: There is no checkbox to disable a source; you must use the 'Find in' toggle.

options.text: No, the integration is read-only
options.explanation: Search scope control is a feature of the search interface, not related to read/write permissions.

questions.question: How quickly does Notion AI typically index new Asana tasks?

options.text: Immediately upon creation
options.explanation: There is a slight delay in indexing to ensure data consistency.

options.text: Within 2 hours
options.explanation: New projects and tasks are usually available in Notion AI searches within 2 hours of creation.

options.text: Up to 72 hours
options.explanation: 72 hours is the maximum time for the full integration to complete, not the indexing time for new content.

options.text: Once the user logs in again
options.explanation: The indexing is automatic and happens in the background.

questions.question: How far back does the integration look for historical data during setup?

options.text: 30 days
options.explanation: The integration has a wider historical window than just 30 days.

options.text: 6 months
options.explanation: The historical data window is longer than 6 months.

options.text: 1 year
options.explanation: Once set up, the integration can find projects and tasks created, viewed, or modified in the past year.

options.text: Indefinitely
options.explanation: The connector is limited to the last year of historical data.

questions.question: Is it possible to connect multiple Asana workspaces to a single Notion workspace?

options.text: Yes, you can connect unlimited workspaces
options.explanation: The current integration limits one Asana workspace per Notion workspace.

options.text: Yes, but only if you are an Enterprise admin
options.explanation: Even Enterprise admins are limited to connecting one Asana workspace to one Notion workspace.

options.text: No, only one Asana workspace can be connected
options.explanation: The system does not currently support connecting multiple distinct Asana workspaces to a single Notion workspace.

options.text: Yes, but you must create a new Notion workspace for each
options.explanation: The limitation is at the workspace level, not the account level.

questions.question: What happens to your Asana data immediately after disconnecting the integration?

options.text: It is permanently deleted from Asana
options.explanation: Disconnecting does not affect data stored within the Asana platform itself.

options.text: It becomes unsearchable in Notion AI
options.explanation: Notion applies the principle of data minimization, making the data unsearchable immediately upon disconnection.

options.text: It is archived for 30 days before deletion
options.explanation: There is no archival period; access is revoked immediately.

options.text: It is sent to your personal email
options.explanation: Disconnecting does not export or email the data.

questions.question: How does Notion store Asana projects and tasks?

options.text: As raw SQL database entries
options.explanation: Notion uses vector databases for efficient semantic search capabilities.

options.text: As embeddings in vector databases like Turbopuffer
options.explanation: These embeddings allow for efficient and accurate searches across different text data.

options.text: As HTML files in a cloud storage bucket
options.explanation: Data is stored in a structured vector format, not as file exports.

options.text: As metadata in a spreadsheet
options.explanation: The storage method is optimized for AI processing and retrieval, not spreadsheet compatibility.

questions.question: Is a Service account mandatory for Enterprise customers to use the Asana connector?

options.text: Yes, it is required to establish the connection
options.explanation: While a token is required for the connection, a Service account is not mandatory.

options.text: No, but it is recommended to access private tasks
options.explanation: Without a Service account, Notion AI may miss 'user-created' private tasks that are not linked to a project.

options.text: Yes, only for reading public tasks
options.explanation: The Service account provides broader access to private data not tied to a project.

options.text: No, it is only for exporting data to Notion
options.explanation: The Service account is for internal reading and processing of data within Notion.
